qa_pairs.question: When using an OnDemand desktop on the Nibi cluster, how do you start Ansys Workbench with GPU acceleration?
qa_pairs.answer: To start Ansys Workbench with GPU acceleration on an OnDemand desktop on the Nibi cluster, you must select a `t4 (15GB)` GPU from the GPU selector pulldown list when configuring your OnDemand desktop session. This action automatically sets up the necessary VirtualGL environment variables. Once your desktop is ready, open a terminal, load the module `StdEnv/2023 ansys/2025R2.04` (or a newer version), and then execute `runwb2`.

qa_pairs.question: What specific environment variable must be manually set on the Nibi cluster to prevent Ansys Fluent from crashing during startup when using the default Intel MPI within an OnDemand Compute Desktop session?
qa_pairs.answer: When using the Nibi cluster, the environment variable `I_MPI_HYDRA_BOOTSTRAP=ssh` must be manually set. If this variable is not configured, Ansys Fluent will crash upon startup when run with the default Intel MPI within OnDemand Compute Desktop sessions.

qa_pairs.question: If Ansys Workbench applications exhibit corrupted graphics when started from a Jupyterhub Desktop, what steps can be taken to resolve this issue by running Workbench in Mesa mode?
qa_pairs.answer: To resolve corrupted graphics in Ansys Workbench applications when using a Jupyterhub Desktop, you can create a custom desktop icon to start Workbench in Mesa mode. First, exit Workbench and open a terminal. Then, copy the default Workbench icon by running `cd ~/Desktop; cp -p $(realpath workbench.desktop) workbench-mesa.desktop`. Next, edit the newly created `~/Desktop/workbench-mesa.desktop` file using a text editor like `nano`, changing all instances of `runwb2` to `runwb2 -oglmesa`. Save the changes, then refresh the Jupyter desktop by pressing `control-R`. The new icon will appear, and double-clicking it will launch Workbench in Mesa mode.
